info.name: AudioDelivery API
info.description:
Complete test suite for the AudioDelivery REST API.

Import this file into Postman, start the API (`dotnet run` from `src/AudioDelivery.Api`), then:
1. Run **Categories** and **Genres** requests first — they use deterministic seeded GUIDs and work out-of-the-box.
2. Use response data to populate the collection variables (userId, artistId, albumId, trackId, playlistId).
3. Work through the remaining folders in the suggested order from `tests/postman/README.md`.

**AlbumType enum** (used in PATCH Album body):
- `0` = Album
- `1` = Single
- `2` = Compilation

variable.description: Base URL of the running API (matches launchSettings.json 'http' profile)
A valid User GUID — populate from GET /api/v1/me or database after seeding
A valid Artist GUID — populate from GET /api/v1/artists or database after seeding
A second valid Artist GUID — used in 'Get Several Artists'
A valid Album GUID — populate from GET /api/v1/browse/new-releases or database after seeding
A second valid Album GUID — used in 'Get Several Albums'
A valid Track GUID — populate from GET /api/v1/albums/{id}/tracks or database after seeding
A second valid Track GUID — used in 'Get Several Tracks'
A valid Playlist GUID — populate by creating a playlist first via POST /api/v1/users/{userId}/playlists
Seeded: 'Music' category — pre-populated and ready to use. Other seeded categories increment the last segment: ...000000000002 = 'New Releases', ...000000000003 = 'Upcoming Releases', etc.

request.description:
GET /api/v1/albums/{id}

Get Spotify catalogue information for a single album.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — AlbumDto
- `404 Not Found` — album does not exist

GET /api/v1/albums?ids=...

Get catalogue information for multiple albums in a single request.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — `{ "albums": [AlbumDto] }`

GET /api/v1/albums/{id}/tracks

Get a paginated collection of an album's tracks.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — array of TrackDto
- `404 Not Found` — album does not exist

PATCH /api/v1/albums/{id}

Partially update an album's details.

**AlbumType enum:**
- `0` = Album
- `1` = Single
- `2` = Compilation

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — updated AlbumDto
- `404 Not Found` — album does not exist

query.description: Optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code filter

request.description:
GET /api/v1/browse/new-releases

Get a paginated list of new album releases featured in Spotify.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — `{ "albums": [AlbumSummaryDto] }`

GET /api/v1/artists/{id}

Get Spotify catalogue information for a single artist.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — ArtistDto
- `404 Not Found` — artist does not exist

GET /api/v1/artists?ids=...

Get catalogue information for multiple artists in a single request.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — `{ "artists": [ArtistDto] }`

GET /api/v1/artists/{id}/albums

Get a paginated list of albums by an artist.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — array of AlbumSummaryDto

GET /api/v1/artists/{id}/top-tracks

Get an artist's top tracks.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — `{ "tracks": [TrackDto] }`

GET /api/v1/artists/{id}/related-artists

Get artists similar to a given artist (based on shared genres).

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — `{ "artists": [ArtistDto] }`

GET /api/v1/browse/categories

Get a paginated list of categories used to tag items in Spotify.

✅ **Uses seeded data — works without setting any collection variables.**

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — `{ "categories": [CategoryDto] }`

GET /api/v1/browse/categories/{id}

Get a single browse category.

✅ **Default `categoryId` variable points to the seeded 'Music' category — works out-of-the-box.**

Other seeded GUIDs:
- `b1000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002` = New Releases
- `b1000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003` = Upcoming Releases
- `b1000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000004` = Made For You

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — CategoryDto
- `404 Not Found` — category does not exist

GET /api/v1/browse/categories/{id}/playlists

Get a list of Spotify playlists tagged with a particular category. Requires at least one playlist to be linked to the category.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — `{ "playlists": [PlaylistSummaryDto] }`

GET /api/v1/genres/seeds

Retrieve a list of all available genre seed values for recommendations.

✅ **Uses seeded data — works without setting any collection variables.**

First few seeded genres (index-based GUIDs `b1000000-0000-0000-0000-{n}`):
- `...000000000001` = Rock
- `...000000000002` = Alternative Rock
- `...000000000003` = Indie Rock

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — `{ "genres": [GenreDto] }`

query.description: Temporary workaround — will be resolved from JWT claims in Phase 8

request.description:
PUT /api/v1/me/library

Save one or more items to the current user's library (max 40 URIs).

**Supported URI types:** `track`, `album`, `artist`, `playlist`

Example URIs:
- `spotify:track:{{trackId}}`
- `spotify:album:{{albumId}}`
- `spotify:artist:{{artistId}}`
- `spotify:playlist:{{playlistId}}`

Multiple items: separate with commas in the `uris` string.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — items saved
- `400 Bad Request` — uris missing

query.description: Temporary workaround — will be resolved from JWT claims in Phase 8

request.description:
DELETE /api/v1/me/library

Remove one or more items from the current user's library (max 40 URIs).

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — items removed
- `400 Bad Request` — uris missing

query.description: Temporary workaround — will be resolved from JWT claims in Phase 8

request.description:
GET /api/v1/me/library/contains

Check whether one or more items are saved in the current user's library.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — LibraryCheckResult with boolean results per URI
- `400 Bad Request` — uris query param missing

GET /api/v1/playlists/{id}

Get full details of a playlist owned by a user, including tracks.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — PlaylistDto
- `404 Not Found` — playlist does not exist

PUT /api/v1/playlists/{id}

Change a playlist's name, description, and public/private/collaborative state. All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — playlist updated
- `404 Not Found` — playlist does not exist

GET /api/v1/playlists/{id}/items

Get full details of tracks in a playlist (paginated).

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — array of PlaylistTrackDto

POST /api/v1/playlists/{id}/items

Add one or more tracks to a playlist.

- `uris` — array of Spotify track URIs (`spotify:track:{guid}`)
- `position` — zero-based insert position; omit to append to the end

**Responses:**
- `201 Created` — `{ "snapshot_id": "..." }`

GET /api/v1/users/{userId}/playlists

Get a paginated list of playlists owned or followed by a user.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — array of PlaylistSummaryDto

POST /api/v1/users/{userId}/playlists

Create a new playlist for a user. Copy the returned `id` into the `playlistId` collection variable to use it in subsequent requests.

**Responses:**
- `201 Created` — PlaylistDto (copy the `id` into the `playlistId` variable)

query.description: Comma-separated result types (required): album, artist, track, playlist

request.description:
GET /api/v1/search

Search for albums, artists, tracks, and/or playlists matching a keyword string. Both `q` and `type` are required.

Try different queries:
- `q=the beatles&type=artist,album`
- `q=summer hits&type=playlist`
- `q=jazz&type=track,album`

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — SearchResultDto
- `400 Bad Request` — q or type missing

GET /api/v1/tracks/{id}

Get Spotify catalogue information for a single track.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — TrackDto
- `404 Not Found` — track does not exist

GET /api/v1/tracks?ids=...

Get catalogue information for multiple tracks in a single request.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — `{ "tracks": [TrackDto] }`

query.description: Temporary workaround — will be resolved from JWT claims in Phase 8

request.description:
GET /api/v1/me

Get detailed profile information about the current user. Temporarily accepts `userId` as a query parameter until JWT authentication is implemented in Phase 8.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — UserProfileDto
- `404 Not Found` — user does not exist

GET /api/v1/users/{userId}

Get the public profile information about any user.

**Responses:**
- `200 OK` — PublicUserDto
- `404 Not Found` — user does not exist
